Маленький принц
Маленький принц is described as an unnamed young human character from a text about The Little Prince. He is encountered by two gryphs and a three-color beetle during his adventures, and sometimes he has conversations with these creatures. In some versions, he discusses topics such as empathy, a chaste lover, and possessions. M.Lenkoov explains the prince's actions in one narrative. Initially, the young prince visits a planet inhabited by an alcoholic individual struggling with alcoholism; he approaches this person curiously to help understand their behavior. Another version describes the prince having an unusual way of dealing with possessions and ideas. There are conflicting descriptions suggesting that M.Lenkoov might be part of multiple versions or retellings of The Little Prince. Throughout his journey, the young prince encounters various characters such as gryphs and a three-color beetle, engages in conversations about abstract concepts like empathy and relationships, questions permanence and ephemeralness in life, and discards a flower he loves, leaving it on Earth to be alone. He travels from his home planet and is fascinated by geography. The core essence of Маленький принц remains as a young human character navigating through life's complexities with interactions and discussions involving other characters and abstract ideas.<SEP>Young prince who travels through the desert and meets a flower.
